context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM calls by creating instrumented versions of all LLM providers that count input and output tokens, leveraging the existing instrumentation system.
key_requirements: Create fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py with token counting logic and metrics collection
Create fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_providers.py with instrumented versions of all LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) that wrap chat methods to count tokens
Create fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py with data models for token usage reporting
Instrument chat and chat_stream methods on all LLM provider classes to track input/output tokens
Use the existing InstrumentedProxy system from core/instrumentation.py for hook-based tracking
constraints: Must use existing instrumentation system (InstrumentedProxy) rather than building custom approach
Token counting must be done at LLM call level per provider-specific models
All existing LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) must be supported with cross-provider compatibility
Implementation must maintain backward compatibility and not change existing APIs
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py contains InstrumentedProxy and benchmarking hook system; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines provider protocols including ChatProvider, StreamingChatProvider; all LLM providers (openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py) implement these protocols
stakeholders: LLM users who want to see token counts in their query responses
System administrators monitoring LLM usage costs

in_scope: Token tracking logic and metrics collection for LLM calls
Instrumented versions of all LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) with token counting
Data models for token usage reporting
out_of_scope: Implementing user-facing UI elements for displaying token counts beyond query responses or API endpoints
Aggregating token usage at a higher level rather than per-LLM call
Adding new APIs or endpoints specifically for token usage visualization

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py — Token tracking logic and metrics collection
fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_providers.py — Instrumented versions of all LLM providers with token counting
fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py — Data models for token usage reporting

assumptions.assumption: Token tracking should be implemented using the existing instrumentation system (InstrumentedProxy)
assumptions.impact: Would need to rearchitect around a different approach

assumptions.assumption: Token counting is provider-specific and needs to happen at the LLM call level
assumptions.impact: Might need to parse responses from all providers

assumptions.assumption: The system should track both input tokens and output tokens for chat operations
Token counting will be done per-LLM call rather than aggregated at higher level
assumptions.impact: Could miss some queries or double-count

assumptions.assumption: Users want to see token counts in their query responses or via an API endpoint
assumptions.impact: Implementation may not meet user expectations for access

approaches.name: Extend Provider Interfaces with Token Awareness
approaches.description: Modify base provider protocols to return token usage metadata alongside core responses, creating new extended interfaces that maintain backward compatibility.
pros: Maintains consistent interface across all providers
Enables comprehensive token tracking without breaking existing code
cons: Requires careful migration strategy to avoid breaking changes
Involves modifying core provider contracts which could impact downstream consumers
Needs dual-interface implementation for backward compatibility
best_for: Production systems requiring robust token tracking with minimal disruption
Organizations with existing codebases that depend on current provider interfaces

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Token Tracking System
approaches.description: Implement a comprehensive instrumentation system using BenchmarkHook to capture and aggregate token usage across all LLM calls, requiring modifications to providers to preserve raw API response objects.
pros: Leverages existing observability infrastructure already present in the codebase
Provides uniform tracking mechanism across all provider backends
Can be toggled on/off with minimal performance overhead when disabled
cons: Requires providers to preserve raw response data instead of converting to simple strings
Breaks current API contracts that return only basic types like str or list[float]
May have significant implementation complexity due to provider-specific handling needed for different backends
best_for: Teams looking to add observability without major architectural changes
Systems already using instrumentation hooks and wanting to extend them with token data

architecture.recommended: Extend Provider Interfaces with Token Awareness
architecture.reasoning: The instrumentation-based approach is infeasible because current LLM provider implementations convert full API response objects (which contain usage metadata) into simple string types before returning, so the token information is already discarded by the time hooks can access it. Additionally, modifying the core ChatProvider protocol would break backward compatibility with existing consumers of these interfaces that expect only str returns. The recommended approach addresses both issues: extending provider interfaces to include optional token tracking without breaking existing behavior (using dual-interface design), and enabling consistent tracking across all providers through the existing abstraction pattern.

key_tradeoffs.backward_compatibility_vs_enhanced_funct: Adding token tracking requires modifying provider interfaces which breaks backward compatibility with existing code that expects simple return types (str, list[float]). The tradeoff is between immediate functionality gains and maintaining existing consumer contracts.
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_overhead_vs_observabilit: Using the existing instrumentation system provides clean observability but requires either changing provider implementations to preserve response metadata or creating wrapper layers. The overhead is minimal but adds complexity to provider code.
key_tradeoffs.provider_unification_vs_specialization: The architecture assumes all providers can be instrumented uniformly, but different LLM backends (OpenAI, Cohere, Ollama) have varying levels of token usage exposure and require custom extraction logic.

technology_considerations: OpenAI SDK integration with token counting via response.usage field
Cohere API usage fields that need parsing from responses or headers
Ollama model calls without built-in token metadata support requiring manual tracking
Instrumentation system hooks in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for observability
LLM provider protocols in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py that currently return simple types
architecture.scope_statement: Modify LLM provider protocols to optionally return token usage metadata, update implementation classes to preserve raw API responses for token extraction, and integrate with existing instrumentation system for comprehensive observability while maintaining backward compatibility.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend LLM Provider Interfaces for Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The current architecture lacks token usage tracking capability across LLM providers, making it impossible to monitor costs or optimize LLM consumption. Existing provider interfaces return simple types (str, list[float]) that discard valuable metadata from API responses.
adrs.decision: Create extended protocol interfaces (TokenAwareChatProvider, TokenAwareEmbeddingProvider) alongside existing base protocols to enable token tracking without breaking backward compatibility
adrs.rationale: This approach maintains full backward compatibility while enabling enhanced functionality. Providers can optionally implement the new token-aware interfaces when needed, and consumers can choose whether to use them. This follows the established pattern of protocol-based abstraction in the codebase.
consequences: Providers must implement both base protocols (for backward compatibility) and extended ones (for token tracking)
Engine logic needs to detect and utilize token-aware providers when available
SDK layer can optionally expose token usage information in Answer objects
No breaking changes to existing code that doesn't require token tracking
alternatives_considered: Modify base interfaces directly - rejected because it would break backward compatibility with all existing implementations
Add custom middleware that intercepts calls after they return strings - rejected because token usage data is already discarded by providers before returning
Implement a separate metrics collection system outside the provider layer - rejected because it duplicates functionality and doesn't integrate well with existing instrumentation patterns

adrs.title: ADR: Instrumentation System Enhancement for Token Usage Aggregation
adrs.context: The existing instrumentation system can track method calls and timing but cannot capture token usage data from LLM providers because that information is lost during provider method execution.
adrs.decision: Extend the BenchmarkHook protocol to include a TokenUsageHook interface that captures and aggregates token information at method call level, leveraging existing InstrumentedProxy infrastructure
adrs.rationale: This approach leverages the proven instrumentation system already in place rather than building new monitoring infrastructure. It integrates seamlessly with the logging stack and allows optional activation through configuration.
consequences: New hook interface requires implementation by providers to capture token data
Engine layer must coordinate between different hook types (timing + token usage)
Token aggregation logic needs to be thread-safe for concurrent query handling
Performance overhead is minimal when hooks are disabled
alternatives_considered: Build entirely new monitoring system - rejected because it duplicates existing instrumentation infrastructure and increases maintenance burden
Modify provider implementations to preserve raw API responses - rejected because this would require changes across all providers with risk of breaking existing code paths
Use global state tracking mechanism - rejected because it introduces complexity around thread safety, context propagation, and makes testing difficult

adrs.title: ADR: Answer Model Extension for Token Usage Metadata
adrs.context: The Answer data model currently has no place to store token usage information collected from LLM operations. Users need access to cost metrics along with their answers.
adrs.decision: Add an optional tokens_used field to the Answer dataclass that can capture aggregated token usage from all related LLM calls during query processing
adrs.rationale: This provides a clean, structured way to expose token costs alongside answers without requiring changes to existing APIs. The field is optional so it doesn't break any existing code paths.
consequences: Answer consumers can access token usage data when provided by the system
Engine logic must aggregate tokens from all LLM interactions and populate this field in final Answer objects
SDK layer needs to expose this information through fitz.ask() and fitz.query() methods if available
Default value is None for backward compatibility with existing answer creation code
alternatives_considered: Create entirely separate response type that includes tokens - rejected because it creates API fragmentation and requires updating multiple call sites
Add token tracking as a completely separate telemetry system - rejected because this duplicates effort and doesn't integrate well with the current Answer-based architecture
Modify core answer generation logic to always compute token usage - rejected because it would make answers less predictable in scenarios where token tracking is disabled

adrs.title: ADR: Provider-Level Token Data Preservation Strategy
adrs.context: LLM provider methods convert full API response objects (which contain token usage) into simple strings before returning, making it impossible for instrumentation hooks to access this data.
adrs.decision: Modify all LLM provider classes to preserve and return raw API response metadata in addition to converted string results when the enhanced protocol is used
adrs.rationale: This approach ensures that providers don't lose token usage information during conversion while maintaining backward compatibility with existing consumers of their simple interfaces.
consequences: All LLM provider implementations need modification to store and return raw response data
Token tracking requires using the new enhanced protocols rather than base ones
Backward compatibility is maintained for users not requiring token usage information
Error handling needs to be robust when API calls fail or don't include expected usage fields
alternatives_considered: Add wrapper functions that intercept and preserve responses - rejected because this would require significant changes to each provider's method implementation
Change all providers to return full response objects instead of strings - rejected because it breaks the established contract for synchronous chat operations across all providers
Implement token extraction at a lower HTTP level - rejected because this is too complex and bypasses existing abstraction layers

components.name: OpenAIChat
components.purpose: Provides OpenAI chat functionality with authentication, model selection, and streaming capabilities

Support streaming chat responses via iterator interface
Manage authentication and API configuration through AuthProvider

Return embedding dimensions information

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wraps target objects to instrument method calls for benchmarking and caching purposes
responsibilities: Wrap target object methods with instrumentation logic
Track method calls through layer/plugin identification
interfaces: InstrumentedProxy.__init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Knowledge Routing Augmented Generation engine for processing queries and generating answers using various retrieval and generation strategies
responsibilities: Load knowledge collections into memory
Perform fast analysis of incoming queries before full processing
Generate comprehensive answers to user questions by routing through appropriate components
Implement caching mechanisms for performance optimization
interfaces: FitzKragEngine.point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK interface for interacting with the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide user-friendly API for pointing source code and querying knowledge bases
Manage collection selection and configuration loading
interfaces: fitz.__init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
fitz.ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

integration_points: OpenAIChat — chat method calls OpenAI SDK which provides usage data in response.usage, but current interface only returns str
CohereChat — makes HTTP calls to Cohere API that may provide usage fields, but wrappers don't expose this information
AnthropicChat — calls Anthropic SDK which could provide token info but current implementation discards it
OllamaChat — makes direct HTTP calls without exposing token usage from responses in current wrapper implementations

responsibilities: Provide user-friendly API for pointing source code and querying knowledge bases
Manage collection selection and configuration loading
interfaces: fitz.__init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
fitz.ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

integration_points: OpenAIChat — chat method calls OpenAI SDK which provides usage data in response.usage, but current interface only returns str
CohereChat — makes HTTP calls to Cohere API that may provide usage fields, but wrappers don't expose this information
AnthropicChat — calls Anthropic SDK which could provide token info but current implementation discards it
OllamaChat — makes direct HTTP calls without exposing token usage from responses in current wrapper implementations

artifacts.purpose: OpenAI provider implementations with their method signatures and interfaces
Base protocol definitions for LLM providers that define the interfaces all implementations must follow
Instrumentation system that enables benchmarking and hook-based monitoring of method calls

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source

phases.name: MVP: Core Token Usage Data Models
phases.objective: Create data models for token tracking without implementing capture logic
deliverables: fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py with TokenUsage and TokenUsageSummary classes
key_risks: Data model schema may not align with actual usage metadata from LLM providers
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage, TokenUsageSummary; print('✓ Models defined')"

phases.name: Instrumented Proxy Extension
phases.objective: Extend benchmark hook protocol to support token usage reporting
key_risks: TokenUsageHook interface conflicts with existing hook system or is not properly integrated into InstrumentedProxy
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.instrumentation import TokenUsageHook; print('✓ TokenUsageHook protocol defined')"

phases.name: Provider Interface Extension
phases.objective: Create token-aware protocols extending existing provider interfaces
deliverables: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py with TokenAwareChatProvider, TokenAwareEmbeddingProvider, TokenAwareRerankProvider, and TokenAwareVisionProvider protocols
key_risks: Token-aware protocols break backward compatibility with existing provider implementations
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import TokenAwareChatProvider; print('✓ TokenAwareChatProvider defined')"

phases.name: Provider Instrumentation Layer Implementation
phases.objective: Implement tracked provider wrappers that capture token usage from LLM APIs
deliverables: fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_providers.py with TrackedOpenAIChat, TrackedOpenAIEmbedding and other provider wrapper classes
key_risks: Instrumentation logic conflicts with existing API response handling or fails to extract usage data from providers like OpenAI/Cohere/Ollama
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_providers import TrackedOpenAIChat; print('✓ TrackedOpenAIChat defined')"

phases.name: Integration into FitzKrag Engine and SDK
phases.objective: Modify engine and SDK to capture and expose token usage in Answer metadata
deliverables: Updated fitz_ai/engines/fitz_krag/engine.py with token tracking integration
Updated fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py with token metadata exposure
key_risks: Answer metadata field may not scale or could break existing Answer serialization logic
phases.verification_command: python -c "import tempfile; from fitz_ai.sdk.fitz import fitz; f = fitz('test', auto_init=False); print('✓ fitz class can be imported and instantiated')"

phases.name: Token Usage Hook Registration & Integration
phases.objective: Create and register token tracking hook to collect usage data from instrumented providers
deliverables: Integration of token tracker into system startup
key_risks: Hook registration may fail silently or not be properly propagated to all instrumented components
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import SimpleTokenTracker; tracker = SimpleTokenTracker(); print('✓ SimpleTokenTracker created and registered')"

risks.description: All LLM providers must be instrumented with token extraction logic
risks.mitigation: Verify that each provider's API response includes usage metadata and modify wrappers to preserve this data during conversion from rich objects to strings
risks.contingency: Implement fallback mechanism to log errors when usage data cannot be extracted and provide default zero-token values
risks.verification: Test that OpenAI returns a response with usage metadata when we call chat() directly

risks.description: Instrumentation system cannot preserve metadata needed for token tracking
risks.mitigation: Modify provider interfaces to return both result and usage info while maintaining backward compatibility through protocol extensions
risks.contingency: Maintain dual interface approach where existing string-returning methods continue working but new tuple-returning ones provide token data
risks.verification: Verify we can still call providers with current interfaces

risks.description: Answer metadata field may not scale well for aggregated token usage
risks.mitigation: Design Answer metadata to store detailed breakdown including per-call token usage alongside total, or provide separate structured logging
risks.contingency: Implement optional fine-grained token tracking in addition to aggregate totals for advanced use cases
risks.verification: Check that Answer dataclass allows adding metadata fields properly

risks.description: Provider-specific token structure differences make unified approach brittle
risks.mitigation: Create provider-specific adapters that normalize usage data into consistent TokenUsage format regardless of underlying API structures
risks.contingency: Implement fallback handling for unknown token structures with logging warnings but graceful degradation
risks.verification: Inspect actual OpenAI response format via manual call or documentation review

risks.description: Inconsistent integration point between engine and SDK layers
risks.mitigation: Ensure hook registration is centralized and shared across both engine and service layers, with clear documentation of expected state management
risks.contingency: Add explicit tests covering both direct engine calls and SDK usage paths to verify consistency in token tracking results
risks.verification: Run unit test verifying both path (engine directly vs fitz SDK) produce same token info

risks.description: Token tracking hook silently fails to register due to early imports
risks.mitigation: Implement robust initialization sequence that ensures hooks are registered before any instrumented calls occur, with startup validation checks
risks.contingency: Add health check endpoint or debug output confirming active hooks at runtime and provide mechanism to manually trigger registration if needed
risks.verification: Verify hooks are active after import

risks.description: API responses from OpenAI/Cohere/Ollama change and break token parsing
risks.mitigation: Implement robust error handling with try-catch blocks around token parsing logic, including fallback to default values when data is missing or malformed
risks.contingency: Add monitoring alerts for unexpected API response structures and create versioned parsing strategies that can handle multiple formats gracefully
risks.verification: Mock external API and assert our wrapper handles various response structures

recommended_contingencies: Implement fallback mechanism to log errors when usage data cannot be extracted and provide default zero-token values
Maintain dual interface approach where existing string-returning methods continue working but new tuple-returning ones provide token data
Design Answer metadata to store detailed breakdown including per-call token usage alongside total, or provide separate structured logging
Create provider-specific adapters that normalize usage data into consistent TokenUsage format regardless of underlying API structures
Add explicit tests covering both direct engine calls and SDK usage paths to verify consistency in token tracking results
Implement robust initialization sequence that ensures hooks are registered before any instrumented calls occur, with startup validation checks
